title: Panel order blink after close panel
author: Apollon-Theos
body_snippet:
After close emptry panel, shortly show first panel, and after switch to panel left from closed.

## Expected Behavior
Show left panel from closed, without any artifacts.

## Current Behavior
Switch to first panel, shortly, then switch to panel left from closed.

## Possible Solution
Read a book from Donald Knuth ? :)

## Steps to Reproduce (for bugs)
1. Open more that 2 panels with tabs inside
2. Close last (right) panel
3. Look at program
4.

## Context
<!--- How has this issu...

iterations.description: The issue describes a UI bug (blinking panel order) that affects user experience when closing panels.

comments.author: Apollon-Theos
comments.snippet:
Close panel "Screenshots" without this effect.
After close "Screenshots" not view this effect.

But, today I work remotely. Maybe over RDP not transferred this effect.

comments.author: Apollon-Theos
comments.snippet:
Today I cannot repeat this effect. Maybe depend of number opened tabs in all panels.
Today I has opened up to 7 tab in 4 panels.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

notes:
AI triage (gemini): The issue describes a UI bug (blinking panel order) that affects user experience when closing panels.
Approach: Investigate the panel closing logic in `ConnectionWindow.cs`, focusing on `OnConnectionTabFormClosing` and `Connection_FormClosing` events. Analyze the sequence of operations for panel re-arrangement and activation after a panel is closed. The fix will likely involve minimizing redraws or layout updates, possibly by strategically using `SuspendLayout()` and `ResumeLayout()` on the `DockPanel` or related controls, or re-ordering operations to ensure a smoother visual transition.
